title: grewia apetala
intro: Grewia apetala, also known as the false sterculia or stinking hog plum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant has been found to have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antidiabetic properties, making it useful for treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and diabetes. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves, bark, and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices that can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
